{{Quote|To be blunt, sir, the man's a snake. He looks to have a finger in every stewpot from Muunilinst to Coruscant and back again. We've already uncovered fifteen separate lines to New Republic financial and commodities interests, and we haven't even scratched the surface yet.|[[Dreyf]]|<ref name="Specter of the Past">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>}}
'''[[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Graemon''' was a civilian financial expert of some standing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] by [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] and carried some heavy influence within the [[Imperial Remnant]]

==Biography==
Though not a military officer, his opinion carried a heavy weight within the [[Imperial Remnant]]. He was one of many to speak out against the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Imperial Fleet]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon's]] proposal for a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|treaty of peace]] between the [[Imperial Remnant]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Through statements made by [[High General]] [[Sutt Ramic]] to Pellaeon, Graemon was also suspected of embezzling Imperial funds. The investigations of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Dreyf]], an [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] officer loyal to and under the orders of Pellaeon, it was found that Graemon was in fact one of three players in a conspiracy concerning the misappropriation and channeling of Imperial funds to the [[Cavrilhu Pirates]] in exchange for [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class starfighters]]; the other two conspirators were [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jaron Kyte]]. Backed by files taken from Disra's office on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], Dreyf's investigation also uncovered numerous illegal connections to New Republic financial and commodity interests. Along with Disra and Kyte, Graemon was later arrested in the wake of the [[Caamas Document Crisis]] of the New Republic and attempted coup of the Imperial Remnant by Disra, [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsman]] [[Grodin Tierce]], and the con-artist [[Flim (Human)|Flim]].

==Behind the scenes==
Though Graemon's appearances in ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' and ''[[Vision of the Future]]'' were written long before later sources introduced the [[Muun/Legends|Muuns]] as the inhabitants of Muunilinst, his description as "a lean man wearing the traditional Muunilinsti banker's shawl and pendant" may be consistent with a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] identifying him as a Muun.
